cooperates.cooperate_desc: What makes him great is that he can extend our initiation - we don't have to wait for teammates and can act as a primary engager
And any other Tank/Roamer with mass crowd control (AoE CC)

emblems.emblem_desc:
Since we've chosen Support Emblems set for enhanced Regen and bonus CD Reduction, we'll pick secondary talents for survivability: Vitality (+225 HP) & Tenacity (+15 Adaptive Defense).

Temporal Reign will allow us to airborne enemies up to three times in the late game and during active team fights.

With Tank Emblems, we already have enough HP and Defense, so we'll prioritize cooldown reduction for Flicker, and Swift (+10% AS) - to perform basic attacks faster and consequently get a small CD Reduction (Guinevere's passive spell).

Temporal Reign will allow us to airborne enemies up to three times in the late game and during active team fights.

equips.equip_desc:
Boots - one of two defensive options, and nothing else.

Thunder Belt - since we're often face-to-face with enemies and have the opportunity to safely harass and stack it, this item is always our number one priority.

The next two items provide us with an incredible amount of stats and bonuses, considering we're almost always on the front line in fights.
For example, at level 11, while surrounded by enemies, we'll get 60 Adaptive Defense from Clocks and another (up to) 40 from Dominant Ice, which also passively grants an additional 40 bonus Magic and Physical Defense.

Oracle - bonus defense, HP, and enhanced regeneration from skills and Clock's passive effect (regen). Combined, all of this makes us extremely tanky at mid-game.

Queen's Wings - massive survivability, CDR, HP, spell vamp, and a bit of nice damage.

Boots for Cooldown Reduction.

Thunder Belt - already covered above (see Main Support build).

BFB - crowd control reduction, mobility, survivability, a bit of damage, and CDR.

Chastise Pauldron - if the enemy team has a strong ADC or any hero that heavily relies on Attack Speed.

Guardian Helmet - you can never have too much HP :)

!!! P.S.
Keep in mind that this build (and main one) is for an ideal scenario that you may not always have - so you'll need to adapt. Don't just mindlessly copy it without understanding the reasoning behind each item.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Atlas's ultimate helps gather all enemies in one spot, making it easy for me to land my combo. When Atlas uses Fatal Links to pull enemies together, I can immediately jump in with my skills and wipe them out.

Tigreal's ultimate is perfect for setting up my attacks. His Implosion pulls and stuns enemies in a small area, giving me the chance to use my knock-up and ultimate to deal massive damage to grouped enemies.

Kimmy helps me finish off enemies that I catch with my skills. When I knock enemies up or use my ultimate, Kimmy can easily aim her Maximum Charge and other attacks to secure kills on the enemies we catch together.

counters.counter_desc: Benedetta can't directly dash onto Guinevere without risking a counterattack, but when she uses her second skill "Shadow Slash" near Guinevere, she stuns her briefly, interrupting Guinevere's combo. This makes Guinevere's burst damage much harder to land. Benedetta's high mobility also lets her dodge skill shots and reposition quickly.

Miya's ranged attacks and quick escape with her ultimate make her a tough opponent for Guinevere. She can kite Guinevere well, avoiding her burst damage and waiting for the right moment to strike back.
Wanwan's high mobility and multiple knockbacks make it very hard for Guinevere to land her combos. Wanwan can dodge Guinevere's crowd control easily and punish her during cooldowns.

dominants.dominant_desc: Before level 4, if the turtle is not on my side, I unlock my first skill and poke the enemy as much as I can while avoiding taking damage from a distance. Once I reach level 4, I can easily win 1v1 fights against almost anyone. If the turtle is on my side, I quickly clear the first minion wave, unlock my second skill for jumping, and look for opportunities to help in early team fights. Most of the time, we manage to get a kill or at least an assist because I can knock enemies up, giving our jungler or mid laner an easy kill. In longer games, I play with the Flicker spell or wait in bushes to catch my prey. :)

emblems.emblem_desc:
I use the Thrill emblem to boost my attack power, maximizing Guinevere's damage potential.

The Festival of Blood emblem helps me quickly regain health by hitting enemies when my HP is low or when I'm trying to escape from team fights. This increases my survivability and allows me to stay longer in fights.

I choose the Lethal Ignition emblem because I don't want my enemies to get surprised by me escaping with low health. It gives me the ability to deal a burst of damage while retreating, securing my safety and putting pressure on my opponents.

This combination perfectly fits Guinevere's aggressive playstyle by balancing high damage output with sustain and strategic escapes.

equips.equip_desc: My first item is Concentrated Energy because it gives spell vamp, helping me sustain myself in fights. The second item is Magic Boots to reduce the cooldown of my first skill, so I can poke and pressure the enemy more often. The third item, Starlium Scythe, works well together with the first item in solo fights against heroes like Terizla or Yu Zhong, as it helps me deal strong basic attack damage after using skills. The fourth and fifth items, Holy Crystal and Divine Glaive, maximize the burst damage of my ultimate, allowing me to take down most enemies quickly. Finally, I choose Winter Crown for protection it gives me a chance to survive when heroes like Johnson (with his stun) or Miya (using Inspire while invisible) try to jump on me. Overall, this build helps me deal damage, sustain in fights, and escape dangerous situations.

hero.hero_overview: Guinevere is a powerful mage/fighter hero known for her strong burst damage and crowd control capabilities. She excels at diving into the backline, eliminating key targets with her combo, and disrupting enemy formations with her AoE skills. Guinevere is especially effective in mid-game skirmishes and excels at ambushing isolated enemies. Her signature ability, Violet Requiem, can wipe out enemies when timed correctly with Spatial Migration. Use her to dominate the battlefield with perfect timing and positioning.
hero.hero_strength:
Exceptional burst damage with strong combos

Effective crowd control with knock-up abilities

Great mobility and chase potential using Spatial Migration

Can snowball easily when ahead

Strong mid-game power spike

Excellent for picking off isolated targets and punishing squishy enemies
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak against mobile marksmen like Miya&wanwan etc. who can kite and escape easily

Vulnerable to ranged poke damage and basic attacks during cooldowns

Cannot lock down targets who dash or blink frequently

Struggles in team fights when enemies stay spread out

Hard-countered by Purify spell

Tip: Guinevere chases, Miya runs... and somehow you're the one who dies.

Tip: If you hear Purify... just walk away and rethink your strategy.

data.recommend: This guide is perfect for players who want to master Guinevere in both solo and team fights. It includes an effective build, battle tips, and hero synergies to help you dominate your matches.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Guinevere works best with Atlas. His strong AoE crowd control (especially his Ultimate, Fatal Links) can pull enemies together and keep them airborne, making it easy for Guinevere to follow up with her Skill 2 and Ultimate combo for massive burst damage. Their synergy is deadly in teamfights, as Atlas sets the stage and Guinevere finishes the job. With the right strategy this is an unbeatable combo

counters.counter_desc: Chou counters Guinevere because his crowd control can interrupt her combo, stopping her Ultimate and knock-up. His mobility lets him catch or peel her easily, preventing her from diving safely. Meanwhile, Guinevere struggles to burst him down quickly, making Chou a strong counter.

dominants.dominant_desc: In teamfights, Guinevere should stay hidden or on the side until key enemy skills are used. Look for an opening, then blink in with Skill 2 to knock enemies airborne. Follow up quickly with Skill 1 and use your Ultimate while they're airborne for max burst damage. Focus on squishy backliners like mages or marksmen. After your combo, use the second blink from Skill 2 to escape or reposition. Use Execute to secure kills or Flicker for safety.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: The matchup between Guinevere and Hanzo is a close one, with both heroes having the potential to dominate. The key to success is to understand each hero's strengths and weaknesses and play accordingly.

counters.counter_desc: His ultimate skill can cancel Guinevere's surprise attacks, and his skill 2 keeps her out of reach.  His skill 1 slows her down, hindering her mobility

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Target Squishies: Guinevere excels at taking down low-HP heroes like mages and marksmen.
 
- Jungle Farming: Use Energy Wave to clear jungle camps for gold and experience.
 
- Teamfight Positioning:  Use your ultimate to initiate teamfights, but be careful not to jump into a losing battle.
 
- Escape and Chase:  Use Spatial Migration to quickly reposition, escape danger, or chase enemies.
 
- Emblem and Battle Spells:  Use the Mage emblem with Rupture, Bargain Hunter, and Impure Rage. Consider Flicker or Execute for battle spells.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Here's a breakdown of why Rupture, Bargain Hunter, and Impure Rage make a strong emblem combination for Guinevere:
 
- Rupture:  Guinevere's strength is in her burst damage and crowd control. Rupture enhances this by providing additional magic penetration, allowing her skills to hit harder and deal more damage, especially against enemies with magic defense. This is crucial for quickly eliminating targets and securing kills.
 
- Bargain Hunter:  Guinevere can struggle with mana management, especially in the early game. Bargain Hunter helps with this by providing mana restoration on successful kills or assists. This allows her to use her skills more frequently, enabling her to dominate lanes and engage in fights more confidently.
 
- Impure Rage:  This talent boosts Guinevere's offensive power by providing increased movement speed after using skills. This enhances her mobility and allows her to reposition quickly, chase enemies, or escape danger. It also helps her get into optimal positions for landing her skills and maximizing their impact.

- Genius Wand: Provides magic power and reduces enemy magic defense.
 
- Concentrated Energy: Offers spell vamp and additional magic power.
 
- Starli Um Scythe: Grants true damage and enhanced basic attacks.
 
- Holy Crystal: Boosts magic power significantly.
 
- Blood Wings: Provides a shield and additional magic power.
 
This build focuses on maximizing Guinevere's burst damage, allowing her to quickly eliminate enemies.

hero.hero_overview:
Guinevere, known as Ms. Violet, is a versatile Fighter/Mage hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB). She is known for her high burst damage, crowd control (CC) abilities, and good mobility. Her playstyle revolves around using her skills to quickly eliminate enemies, especially those with low HP.
Guinevere hails from the prestigious Baroque family, renowned for their fencing skills and beauty. However, she was more drawn to magic than traditional fencing. Her innate magical talents led her to the Magic Academy, where she excelled in combining mental perception with super energy, creating unique magical effects she dubbed "Magic Superpower."
hero.hero_strength:
Guinevere is considered a strong hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB). She excels in several areas:
 
High Damage Output: Guinevere's skills deal significant magic damage, allowing her to quickly eliminate enemies, especially those with low HP. Her passive, Super Magic, further enhances her damage and provides HP regeneration.
 
Powerful Crowd Control: Guinevere has multiple crowd control (CC) abilities, including slows, knock-ups, and an ultimate that can knock enemies airborne multiple times. This makes her effective at disrupting enemy formations and controlling teamfights.
 
Good Mobility: Her Spatial Migration skill grants her a blink ability, allowing her to quickly reposition, chase enemies, or escape danger.
 
Versatility: Guinevere can be played in multiple roles, including Fighter, Mage, and Roamer, making her adaptable to different team compositions.
 
However, she also has weaknesses:
 
Low Durability: Guinevere is quite fragile and relies heavily on her skills to survive. She can be easily countered by heroes with high burst damage or strong CC abilities.
 
Skill Dependency: Her effectiveness relies on her skills being available, and she can be vulnerable if they are on cooldown.
 
Counterable: Certain heroes and items can negate her strengths, such as those with anti-CC abilities, high magic defense, or lifesteal.
 
Overall, Guinevere is a powerful hero with high damage output, good mobility, and strong crowd control. However, her low durability and reliance on skills make her vulnerable to counters.
hero.hero_weakness:
Guinevere, while a powerful hero, has some notable weaknesses:
 
Low Durability:  Guinevere is quite fragile and relies heavily on her skills to survive. She can be easily taken down by heroes with high burst damage or strong crowd control abilities.
 
Skill Dependency: Her effectiveness relies on her skills being available, and she can be vulnerable if they are on cooldown. If her skills are on cooldown, she becomes easily targeted and can be quickly eliminated.
 
Counterable: Certain heroes and items can negate her strengths. For example, heroes with anti-CC abilities can disrupt her combos, while items like Athena's Shield and Oracle can reduce her magic damage output.
 
Limited Teamfight Control: While her ultimate can provide some teamfight control, it's not as effective against heroes with high mobility or those who can easily escape the area of effect.
 
Difficult to Master: Guinevere's playstyle requires precise timing and accurate skill usage. Mastering her combos can be challenging for new players, and even experienced players can make mistakes that lead to her downfall.

spell.spell_desc: Battle spells like Flicker or Execute offer more synergy with Guinevere's abilities. Flicker allows for increased mobility, helping her escape danger or chase enemies. Execute provides an extra burst of damage to finish off low-HP targets, maximizing her kill potential.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game

Remember that you don't always have to kill the enemy fighter in the early game, the most important is to keep up with the opponent's farm. You need to be moderately agressive and avoid gangs by the enemy team. Enemy jungler may gang you around minute 1:30 to 2:00 . 

But also, if enemy team has heroes like Selena, Natalia, Khufra, and Kagura; you can even experience surprise gangs before that. Shortly, have map awareness and try to avoid gangs.

Coming to 1vs1 ,  always try to be the first one to reach the lane. Therefore, you can start your game with a poke by your 1st skill. When you Target the first minion wave with your 1st skill, hit the biggest and strongest one. If you manage to reach lvl 2 before your opponent does , you will be greatly advantageous. You will have 1 big Minion left with you and you will together deal damage to enemy fighter.  However, don't forget that point-balnk damage trade is not applicable against every hero( example, you can't do this against Yu-Zhong, it is impossible).

Try to deal damage to the minions and the enemy fighter at the same time with your basic attacks, which deals AOE damage.

Coming to the turtle fight, follow your tank or Target enemy jungler or mid laner alone.(Jungler is better).

Mid Game
In mid game(Mins 5-10), gang the opponent's mid lane. If the opponents have mages with low mobility, you can easily kill them with your own mid laner. 

If you desteoy exp lane tower, gang the Gold lane and target the enemy marksman. 

In games that you farm well, you can also try solo ambushes for enemy junglers.(Execution spell is crucial here).


Late Game
Mainly follow your tank. Don't go solo and die. You can sell one of your items to buy immortality or winter throne.(Don't sell your anti-heal item).

You can carefully try to solo-kill enemy squishes that don't have purify or defensive items.

Equipments in this guide gives Guinevere tankiness and potential to melt down tanky opponent heroes at the same time. 

Assassin emblem is needed to gain magic penetration(+14). 
Rupture is used to support your penetration bonus(+5).
Tenacity is preferred to increase survival skills
Concussive Blast is chosen to deal more damage. Other damage dealing options are not recommended, because in this guide's build, she will use some tank items which give her high HP, and which works best with Concussive Blast. Because Concussive Blast's damage is based on your maximum HP.

equips.equip_desc:
Arcane boots and Cursed Helmet help you boost your magic penetration.
Cursed Helmet(+9 magic penetration)
Arcane boots(+10 magic penetration)

When we combine this with our emblem set, our Total magic penetration reaches up to +38(19+19). This is almost equal to a whole magic defence item. And the best thing about this build is the fact that you also get tankier while having +38 magic penetration.

Glowing wand limits the lifesteal of enemies and deals true damage. And it gives you magic power. 

Cursed Helmet gifts you +1200 HP( which helps Concussive Blast to be effective), +9 magic penetration, and real damage bonus for every second you are close to an opponent hero.

When Glowing Wand's and Cursed Helmet's real damage bonus  are combined, it melts down the enemy exp laner and tanks. This build may not guarantee you to solo kill an enemy tank or fighter in late game, but I ensure you that in a teamfight, Guinevere with this build will be the among the most ones to deal damage to tank heroes.

Antique Cuirass will help you to survive against physical burst heroes.(Saber,Chou,Hilda,Martis, etc.)

Athena's Shield similarly will help you to survive against magical burst heroes.(Eudora, Kadita, Julian, etc.)

Queen's Wings will boost your survivability and will give you adaptive attack.


These last three items mentioned will also help you to deal more damage with Concussive Blast, because all of the three provides extra maximum HP.


It is also important to note that while this build's main purpose is to fight heroes with high HP and stamina, you can also kill squishy heroes alone with full combo if they do not buy any defensive item.( Example: Layla, Lesley, Pharsa, Luo-yi, Aomon, Xavier, etc.)

Antique Cuirass + Queen's Wing combo provides you damage reduction when their lassives are activated. When these two are combined with Tenacity, Guinevere will be close to be immune to physical burst damage.
equips.equip_title: Fight off Tanky Enemies While Being Tanky

And you have the Cursed Helmet+Genius Wand combo, which is similar to the our item combo in the first build. Genius Wand provides essential magical penetration, so you will not feel the absence of the Arcane Boots.  If the opponent team has a attack speed based hero in the exp lane and a attack speed based marksman in the Gold lane, this build might be preferred to the first one, safely.

Winter Crown is used to avoid dangerous situations.

Also, this build has less HP bonus compared to the first one, Concussive Blast May deal less damage.

And, this build is weaker against physical burst heroes (Chou, Saber , etc.) compared to the first one.

hero.hero_overview: First of all, Guinevere is a versatile hero, as she is the first mage/fighter hero in the game. She best shines in the exp lane.
hero.hero_strength: Her early game is strong. She can safely poke the opponent exp laner while also farming. She is usually advantageous against many fighters in the early game. She can be a gamechanger with the ally tank in the first turtle fight. If she gets fed in the early game, her damage can seriously hurt in the mid game.
hero.hero_weakness:
However, she is kind of weak in late game. Meanwhile being able to fight usually on her own in early game and mid game, she definitely needs help from the ally tank or support to land a succesful combo in the late game.


Leaving apart the context about laning and game duration, and coming to the heroes, she is weak again sudden burst and CC. Especially those CCs which are faster than hers. In my guide, I will show you a new innovative build to cope with that. I am hoping to change the perception of the Guinevere users who only builds her full magic burst damage.

data.recommend: Here is a new approach to Guinevere, hope my guide helps!

spell.spell_desc:
Although flicker became a popular spell for her lately, execution is mostly a better option to choose, especially as an exp laner. 

It is crucial to understand that she is not originally a tank, meaning that it is not her prior responsibility to engage in the enemy team with flicker and catch multiple of them.


Having execution spell in your backpack will always intimidate your opponents in the early game. As mentioned before, Guinevere is mostly advantageous in the early game damage trades, but she needs execution spell to finish up her opponents. Recently, fighters with vegneance spell are very popular, and using the execution spell is a great way to çöpe with them.

This spell deals true damage based on the enemey's lost HP. So , you should cast this spell at the end of your combo rather than the beginning. cooperates.cooperate_desc: * Atlas: His ultimate, "Fatal Links," pulls enemies together, making them easy to hit with Guinevere's skills.
* Tigreal: His ultimate, "Roar," stuns enemies, setting up perfect targets for Guinevere's combos.

counters.counter_desc:
Wanwan's second skill, "Shadow Doppelganger," is a powerful counter to Guinevere for several reasons:

 * Mobility: Shadow Doppelganger allows Wanwan to quickly reposition and avoid Guinevere's devastating combos, particularly her knock-up and stun chain.

 * Dodge: The skill can be used to dodge incoming skillshots, including Guinevere's 'Violet Requiem' and 'Ion,' making her difficult to hit.

 * Punish: After dodging Guinevere's attacks, Wanwan can quickly retaliate with her own burst damage using 'Crossbow of Ten Thousand Miles,' potentially turning the fight in her favor.

* Unparalleled Mobility: Fanny's signature ability allows her to traverse the map with incredible speed, making her difficult to catch and allowing her to quickly gank lanes or escape from danger. This mobility is especially effective against Guinevere, who relies on landing her full combo to deal significant damage. Fanny's ability to quickly reposition and avoid Guinevere's attacks disrupts this strategy.
* Lancelot: His dashes and high attack speed make him a difficult target for Guinevere to lock down.
* Benedetta: Her high mobility allows her to easily dodge Guinevere's attacks and punish her with her own burst damage.
* Akai: Akai's ultimate can also imprison Guinevere, preventing her from escaping or initiating her combo.

dominants.dominant_desc:
While using Guinevere as roam, positioning and timing are important. 

Make sure to aim your second skill correctly. 

*When the enemies are far from the range of your second skill, use flicker. If you use flicker to get to the enemies, don't just aim the flicker. Timing is everything. Fist, use second skill then at .05 click flicker. It's a tricky combo but with practice it will be a piece of cake. Combo: 2-F-P-1-3-2
dominants.dominant_title: * Prioritize High-Value Targets:    * Focus: Identify and eliminate key enemy threats (e.g., carries, supports with strong disables).

emblems.emblem_desc:
* Assassin Emblem: Provides the core stats Guinevere needs: Physical Attack, Physical Penetration, and Movement Speed. This boosts her damage output and allows her to quickly rotate around the map.

 * Conclusive Blast: This talent perfectly complements Guinevere's burst damage. After using a skill (Energy Wave or Spatial Migration), your next basic attack deals significant Magic Damage to nearby enemies. This extra burst can secure kills during ganks or finish off low-health enemies.

This combination maximizes Guinevere's damage potential and movement speed, making her a potent force on the roam.

equips.equip_desc:
This is an excellent build for roaming Guinevere. It strikes a perfect balance between damage, survivability, and utility.
Strengths:

 * Mobility: Tough Boots provide essential movement speed for quick rotations around the map.

 * Burst Damage: Concentrated Energy significantly boosts Magic Power, increasing Guinevere's burst damage potential.

 
 * True Damage: Demon Hunter Sword inflicts true damage on low HP enemies, making it easier to secure kills.

 * Defense: Athena's Shield provides Magic Resistance and a shield that absorbs damage, enhancing survivability against magic damage.

 * Crowd Control and Damage Amplification: Winter Crown provides Cooldown Reduction and reduces the enemy's Magic Resistance, making your skills more effective and your attacks more potent.

* Mobility: Tough Boots provide essential movement speed for quick rotations around the map.

 * Burst Damage: Concentrated Energy significantly boosts Magic Power, increasing Guinevere's burst damage potential.

 * Mana Regen: Oracle provides mana regeneration, crucial for spamming skills during rotations and ganks.

 * Survivability: Athena's Shield and Immortality provide a strong defensive layer, making Guinevere more resilient to enemy attacks.

 * Damage Amplification: Holy Crystal significantly increases Magic Power, further enhancing Guinevere's damage output.

hero.hero_overview: Guinevere is a high-risk, high-reward hero who can be incredibly impactful in the right hands. Mastering her skills and understanding her strengths and weaknesses is crucial for victory.
hero.hero_strength:
Reasons why Guinevere is good for roaming:

* Unexpected Gank Potential: Guinevere's high mobility and burst damage can catch enemies off guard during ganks. Her ability to quickly close the distance and unleash her combo can secure crucial early kills.

 * Strong Crowd Control: Her ultimate, "Violet Requiem," can disrupt enemy teamfights and create openings for your team, even when roaming.

 * Objective Control: Guinevere's mobility allows her to quickly rotate to objectives like turtles and Lord, helping your team secure crucial advantages.
* High Burst Damage: Guinevere can quickly eliminate squishy targets with her powerful combos.

 * Strong Crowd Control: Her ultimate, "Violet Requiem," can knock up multiple enemies, disrupting teamfights and creating openings for her team, Further more with her last year revamped where she can stack up 3 petals so she can knock up her enemy without the use of her second skill. 

 * High Mobility: Her skills allow her to quickly close the gap on enemies and escape from danger.

 Shaping from danger tips:
1. Her second skill can summon a temporary clone, though it doesn't move it can be used for blocking enemy skills like flame shot, Layla's first skill etc. 

Did you know that you can also blocked Johnson's ultimate with the use of her clone? 

2. While someone is chasing you and you were in the jungle, don't hesitate to use your first skillon jungle monsters or the hero that is chasing you. Her 1st skill can reduce the cool down of any skills to 1sec if she hit the target. 

3. Her second skill can leap through walls which allows you to get away from the danger. Just make sure aim it correctly.
hero.hero_weakness:
* Squishy: Guinevere has low health and can be easily killed by burst damage herself.

 * Skill-reliant: Her effectiveness heavily relies on landing her skills accurately.

 * Vulnerable during Ultimate: While casting "Violet Requiem," She's invulnerable to stuns, but it also make her stand in that position for 2seconds so she can be easily targeted.

data.recommend:
How to use Guinevere as roam? 

This comprehensive guide provides valuable insights for using Guinever as roam for those who wants to use her as roam.

spell.spell_desc:
When roaming as Guinevere, Flicker is generally considered the superior battle spell compared to Flameshot. Here's why:

 * Escape and Chase: Flicker provides crucial mobility.

   * Escape: If a gank goes wrong or you're caught out of position, Flicker allows you to quickly disengage and avoid enemy retaliation.

   * Chase: It enables you to close the gap on fleeing enemies, increasing your gank success rate.

 * Combo Enhancement: Flicker can be seamlessly integrated into Guinevere's combos.

   * You can use Flicker to reposition yourself during your "Spatial Migration" (2nd skill) for better positioning or to extend your reach for a more impactful "Violet Requiem" (ultimate).

 * Versatility: Flicker is a highly versatile spell that can be used in various situations, providing both offensive and defensive utility.

In Summary:
Flicker's mobility and versatility make it a more valuable asset for a roaming Guinevere. It enhances her gank potential, improves her survivability, and allows for more dynamic gameplay.

Disclaimer: Make sure to practice the second then flicker combo, before using it on real game.

hero.hero_overview: Guinevere is a powerful mage and fighter known for her burst damage, crowd control, and versatility in both the mid and late game. She excels at dealing magic damage while providing strong utility to her team with her crowd control abilities. Guinevere is typically played in the mid lane but can also be used as a roaming or support hero in other roles.
hero.hero_strength:
High Burst Damage: Guinevere can deal significant magic damage, especially when using her abilities in combination with item builds focused on magic power.

Crowd Control (CC): With her second skill and ultimate, Guinevere excels at disrupting enemy movements, making her a strong presence in team fights.

Mobility: Guinevere can reposition herself quickly using her skills, especially when combined with items that boost her movement speed.

Versatility: She can be built as a burst mage/tanky.
hero.hero_weakness:
Squishy: Despite her ability to deal a lot of damage, Guinevere can be quite fragile. She relies on her positioning and skill usage to avoid getting caught.

Skill-Dependent: Her damage output is heavily reliant on landing her skill shots, particularly her second skill and ultimate. Missing them can severely reduce her effectiveness.

Vulnerable to Crowd Control: Guinevere is susceptible to being locked down by enemy CC, which can prevent her from performing her combo and engaging in fight.

spell.spell_desc: The Execute spell is a finishing move spell that deals true damage to an enemy hero whose HP is below a certain threshold. It is typically used to secure kills on low-health enemies, especially when they're trying to escape.
